text: My experience is too bad with T mobile. I hope it should be 0 star option for t mobile. Very very bad customer service and they are taking money from people for no reason. They keep sending me bill with my name and the amount is increasing day by day. Not helping me at all. I’m very disappointed and this thing is giving me headaches

I switched to TMobile in July 2021, they failed to tell me that the coverage in my area is bad, it's been a loosing battle for me, I was told if I switch back to my previous telephone provider I have to pay TMobile a $500 fee, I think it's wrong,so I am being charged for a service they fail to provide, I will not recommend TMobile to anyone who lives in Boone or Wilkesboro NC.
Just a terrible company both their service and customer service were terrible the whole time I had them will never go back.

T-Mobile has done at not a thing to correct my situation made multiple phone calls been told multiple things the end result not a thing has been done but wasted more of my time I've been a T-Mobile customer for 6 years and I will not be one anymore I will not be treated the way they treat people anymore you have lost a customer due to your inability to follow through with your end of the deal I'm a paying customer and was treated very unfairly and sold a phone that was defective and they won't take any responsibility for it shame on you T-Mobile

I’ve been trying to cancel my 30 free trial on the hot spot. Worst product ever, I have to turn off the hot spot to use the internet on my phone. Been on hold for a combined total of 5 hrs. I started the free trial on  December 30 and have a bill due on January 21. I’m no rocket scientist but that’s not 30 days? T mobile is the worst. Got thing I have 1.5 million followers on YouTube guess I’ll start sharing my experience on there!

Terrible network: 4g, 4g lte, 5g, and 5g uc.  So, I've been with T-Mobile for 9 years now.  I was on a Samsung Galaxy Note 9 and recently switched to new Google Pixel 6 Pro to be able to access 5G because coverage was spotty around Atlanta, GA.  I have been extremely patient hoping they would get their network built out and made reliable. Nope. 5G is worse than 4G.  Massive disappointment.

Absolute worst service I've ever had. I missed out on a job opportunity because the hiring manager called me three times and couldn't get through. I randomly get voicemails from calls that I'd missed days before.

The only answer is to sock it to their stock holders until they tank or change their tune.

Worst customer service ever!!!  Two hour waits snd they never answer.  No chat option.  Rates are good, But if you can’t talk to anybody and you always have to travel down to the city to get service at the store it’s not worth it. They’re a bunch of Nazis from Germany that own this company.  Strictly bought out sprint NEXTEL for the money. There is zero customer service I am leaving T-Mobile after only two months.

Horrible I would go else where all the other reviews I read all pretty much sum up what I have been on the phone for 2 days now with t mobile and still no resolution everytime you talk to customer service you are on hold forever and if they don't like what you say or how you say it they just hang up poor customer service poor management don't waste your time

T-Mobile has been horrible....we've had numerous problems with the phones as well as the plans...however the biggest problem was the charges.
I was supposed to be charged $185.84 for 5 lines...unlimited business...which I was for the first month.  The very next month, they autocharged my CC $358.34!!!?  After HOURS on phone support (and about 10 hangups LITERALLY) I finally got a good customer rep.  Many just hung up on me, and this seems par for the course.  They finally got things sorted out and assured me that my account was "Fixed"....  At the same time they 'revamped' my plan, some of our numbers stopped working...so more time with T-Mobile because the phones stopped being registered on the network..This happens like EVERY time I call in to have them 'fix' my bill (which is 3 times now), the phones stop being registered on the network, and after 2 hours with billing, I go through the SAME CRAP with tech support!  Fast foward the next month, and the charge goes up to $384.34?!?!  The rep then proceeds to tell me that each line "should" cost me $75 per phone, and that I'm getting a major discount and they have to 'restructure' my bill YET AGAIN...this is a MONTHLY thing it seems.  At least this time they gave me a $80 discount for my 3 hours on the phone (although now my phone STILL doesnt work, and I have to go to T-mobile to supposedly replace it)??? Mind you there was NOTHING wrong with my phone before my call to fix the bill (again)!!!!  If T-Mobile keeps screwing up my plans and my phone, I will DITCH them...so far, they're on par with Frontier Communications and AT&T for absolutely crappy billing!  These last two phone companies are approaching criminal...well, Frontier long since passed that boundry since they've been sued in MANY states for illegally collecting 'taxes' that they illegally put in their pocket.  Why can't ANY phone company seem to have their )(*# together?  It's supposed to be ONE FLAT BILL, and it seems to change all the time, as do the rates/quotes.
Will see what happens in Feb...ugh.

BAD FOR BUSINESS!
Horrible customer service. All you get is a fake smile and a run around. The services they sell you will not work. Employees at the stores either cannot help or just do not care. T mobile does care about their customers. STAY AWAY FROM T MOBILE!

Absolutely horrible service. It took me an hour of waiting just to get my Sims card changed which only took 2 minutes to do. I hate the fact that once you do get the new Sims card from an old sprint account it always shows you voice-mail. The visual voice-mail doesn't even work. The Tmobile towers are slow,, and when you have 5g network you may as well have 3g because it's so slow.

T Mobile is absolutely the worst service you can experience...You can't even get service because they're continually transferring you...and the wait is...or was for me...and hour and a half at one point, but, only because I finally hung up. I'm stuck now between services because I'm afraid they're still going to take their payment so I can't afford to start another service (payment).

I didn't even want to leave one star but couldn't access this space without clicking on it.  T-Mobile in the Edison Mall in Fort Myers, Florida, took $65 for a SIM card which they assured my son would work.  They spent approximately three hours assuring us that firstly they were experiencing difficulty with the network, then that no one was available, then saying it wouldn't work at all but they refused to refund the money.  $65 for time-wasting, incompetent service.  Avoid this company at all costs!
Incompetent. Barely speak English and no clue what they are doing. Worthless. Bravo Tmobile. Just sucking money from Americans.
What can I say, worst customer service, worst company ever!

I spent 3+ hours on hold yesterday and transferred 5 times with each of their "expert" reps. I had to inform them of FCC regulations because they are clueless. Then they had the nerve to call me at 12:23 AM. Another hour this morning talking to a "supervisor" who was equally as incompetent. When I requested to speak with her supervisor how is it that she was the designated one with no one above her?

We had had T-Mobile for a number of years with no complaints.  However, after we switched providers, we received a bill for a full month's service, which should not have been.  We only used a week's worth of service, which we would've been more than happy to have paid for.  I called and spoke with three (3) different CSRs, at different times, all who said that they understood what I was saying and who said that they would get the matter resolved for us.  After spending many hours on the phone with them, time I'll never get back, we've started getting notices from a collection agency.  Are you kidding me?  I said that we're willing and able to pay for the services (1 week's worth of time) that we used, but that we will not pay for 3 weeks worth of time that we didn't.  That is highway robbery and just plain wrong.  T-Mobile, you need to make this right immediately.

I've ordered a new router with a T-mobile internet. It's been 3 weeks but I have never gotten the product. You cannot check the status of your order on the website. I called, and I am on hold for over an hour. Terrible customer service.

Today I had to call three times. I was promised a call back, they called me back and placed me on hold for another half hour. After I called again, a nice guy tried to help me with finding the new phone that was supposed to arrive. Instead of e mailing the tracking number he e mailed the return label for my old phone. Here I am waiting for a third phone call back. They make me deal with UPS , but I didn't ship the phone, so why don't they deal with them. I wasted my whole day for a simple question. I am a T Mobile customer for a very long time about 16 years, but this is totally out of line.
611 customer service autoresponse is bad and it needs improvement.

they have the absolute worst customer service. you wait online or on the phone for literally hours with no resolution. you just get passed around and it usually in another country so half the time the person you speak to cannot understand you or just apologizes profusley with no resolution. they say they will email you with tracking information on products  you order and they dont and it never shows up so you have to call again and repeat yourself over and over. I have been with sprint for over 20 years and now Im ready to switch to another company.

Absolutely AWFUL CUSTOMER SERVICE 
To long of a short but I am finally getting a call back 10.5 hrs later....and she still put me on hold!!
It's 1:40am 
I asked him where he was located he said the Philippines!!
22 yrs with this company
We are done!!
STAY AWAY FROM TMOBILE!!!!

By far the worst company I have ever had to deal with.
Bait and switch sales tactics. Incomident overseas staff. Over charge for plan after 1st month. Save yourself and go anywhere but to T mobile.

I have been trying for a week to transfer 4 lines from ATT to T Mobile but their customer service is beyond awful - it is so bad it rates below the worst you could imagine. It’s phony with poorly speaking “experts” reading a tiresome script but having no clue as to how to solve the issue - it’s unbelievable and then all the T Mobile managers in the US just hide behind the horrible call centers - I’ve never experienced worse or more frustrating experience while trying to buy something from a company - they need to try to call themselves and see what we all are complaining about - I’ve been with ATT for over 20 years and just decided to give T Mobile a chance but that has turned out to be a huge waste of time - hours on the phone and now they have all of my personal information including drivers license and credit card numbers - I’m very concerned - they also pulled a bait and switch with the final contract showing a different figure than it should have bee  -that”s pure fraud and someone better help me soon or I’m going to be forced to complain to a higher agency

I am giving an extra star because I actually have service that works. Though I am in total disagreement with T-Mobile's business practices & promotional claims. They DO NOT pay off your phone when you switch your phones to them. YOU are required to pay off your phone & then submit a reimbursement through a complicated system that takes hours to execute until you find the right department to help you. You do not get a cash reimbursement. You get a virtual debit card (no cash advances). This virtual card expires in 6 months, so be careful.   There is a lot of fine print that doesn’t appear in print that you learn as you go along. My online customer experience has not been bad as most others have complained about. But my in store visits have been infuriating. Constant run around & wasted visits to get even the simplest things accomplished.   I am now paying 1/2 of what I was paying with AT&T, I received $1000 on a virtual card. My Netflix is getting paid for. I don’t care about Paramount & AppleTV.  So all this I have accomplished but at a very high emotional cost.  Be prepared.

T mobile sucks!! Went in today to switch service to T mobile they claimed to have ported my number over and when I told them it wasn’t working he claimed to call in and make a call from it and said it worked. But when I left and got home I tried to make a call and it said it wasn’t in service and when I called customer service it said they would call me back in 2 hrs but NEVER did and my phone still doesn’t work. Switching was a BIG mistake.

It is excruciatingly painful to purchase a plan online. When filling out the forms, you're not told what was wrong with the form (e.g. which fields are missing/wrong), and the fields may be wrong for arbitrary reasons (valid postcode not found which can be resolved by refreshing and filling out all the forms from the start, or because you're using a non-US credit card). Even Common App applications are way simpler.

Their customer service is horrendous is worse than dealing with a garbage dumpster man then getting any kind of customer service or technical support whatsoever I would never recommend T-Mobile to anyone nor would I recommend Google or Paramount plus to anyone is nothing but a rip-off operation that you need to abolish we technically call it terrorism but they're getting away with the Bucks

edits.text: And now approximately 36 hours later than i was told I would of already had a replacement Phone, this is what i have discovered after calling the store to where it was to of been delivered but not until tomorrow at maybe possibly at closing time 3.2.2021. So I'm not happy nor satisfied with being  misled to believe it would be done before i thought to post this.

text:
Poor service in Southwest Florida, 941 area code. Been with T-mobile about 4 months, purchased phone from T-mobile. Dropped calls, always being told I'm braking up.

Wasted hours both at store and on hold with tech support.

Seems problems can't be fixed.

Customer service over the phone 611, with T-Mobile, and  “Megan”, including the technical support, has gone above and beyond to do their job. 
They are very…. kind people who want to help in any capacity they know how. 
It’s greatly appreciated. 
And sadly they do not follow up with a survey. The time I want to report how impressed I am with the service I was given. 
So here I find myself searching for any kind of way to let others know my gratitude I feel tonight. Not often do I feel overwhelmed like this from the sincerity of another.
Thank You T-Mobil for employee these wonderful people. I hope you take great care of them!!

T-mobile's customer service is horrible. Most of the time you can't talk to anyone in the United States and their wait time is usually 2 hours. When you do talk to someone out of the country, they either don't do what you asked them to do or the call is dropped. Then if you go to the T-Mobile store, they say they can't help you change anything on your account & tell you to call customer service. It is so frustrating. The store associates should be able to change what you need changed on your account and help alleviate your problem.

Also they charged me $146 for a router to boost their service. When I returned it, they never refunded my money, which they said they would. Customer service (when you can get through to them) said they'd send an email telling me how to get my money back, but they never sent an email. Don't switch to T-Mobile - their customer service is the worst I've ever seen and they lie to you.

We switched to T-Mobile on 10/31/21. There was a great deal to move our Sprint account over and upgrade our phone. We owed a little over $300 on our old phones which I paid in full while we were at the store in Middleburg, Florida. We received the phones quicker than expected and were very happy. BUT we never received a shipping label to return our old phones. Just after Thanksgiving I went back to the store to see if I could return our old phones because the NEW phones were now showing up on my bill. The store told me that it had been entered wrong and apologized, they stated they would call me with a resolution-- which they never did-- I waited several weeks. It is now after Christmas and I had to call customer service at T-Mobile. It sounded like they understood the issue and the supervisor promised me a call today. She called me back-- but thought she was calling because of a charge-- she had NO IDEA that we even spoke yesterday and did not remember my situation so I had to go thru this --AGAIN. Finally she said she understood and basically I have to go back to the store. That's hilarious because the store told me I have to wait until "Corporate escalates the issue". SO -- lesson learned on 10/31/21, I paid off iphone x phones that I will never use again at a cost of $300 just so they could CHARGE me for the new phones. Yes, this is bait and switch. It is disappointing when companies so not or worse can NOT fix THEIR mistake! I would think twice before going to T-Mobile. I will be going back to the store after 1/1/22 when they open to turn these phones back in to use our old phones and then we will go to a company that is not trying to scam people!

Do not deal with T-Mobile.

First, they are thieves. I ordered the 5G Gateway for internet access on Dec 20, 2021. T-Mobile added my "new line" on Dec 22, 2021, but I had not yet received the Gateway. I received the Gateway on Dec 27, 2021. I am being charged for one month of service for the period Dec 22, 2021 - Dec 22, 2021.

T-Mobile is charging me for a month of service from Dec 22 to Dec 22 for service when I did not have the Gateway.

Second, T-Mobile helplines are the worst I have ever dealt with. When you call you are informed that there is a 2-hour wait but,  at least, they let you specify that you want a callback. When you finally get to talk to an "expert" you find that their accents are terrible and they are very, very difficult to understand. The only thing they seem to be able to do is to flip their cards and ask questions. It took me two days and almost 6 hours on the phone to get my T-Mobile account properly set up so I could set up my account and finally get the Gateway to work. Note that this is one week after the end of my monthly bill which is for the period of Dec 22, 2021 - Dec 22, 2021.

I will be dropping T-Mobile as soon as I can find the right equipment to switch to Consumer Cellular.

Just absolutely avoid this company!!! I have been with Sprint for almost 20 years. Now with this little merger of theirs, I have had nothing but the worst customer service ever!!! I am almost 100% they train their call takers to be horrible!!! Choose any other company besides this!!! You were warned. I will not recommend this company and I do plan on leaving this company as soon as possible!

So very bad experience by cross county store the stuff so very rude and gives me wrong information abut all deals the dealer sales Rep ....363 he is a big liar he never help I had switched 4 liens from At&t to T mobil I get only 2 liens service no body like to fix my problms

I’ve paid insurance on my phone for over a year. Needed to make a claim and too frustrated to repeat the whole ordeal. Five stores, driving all over town, waiting in lines. Final result was a replacement phone for $99 deductible, on a phone that only has cracked screen which would have been a $29 deductible. $70 difference, not going to bankrupt me, but the insurance payment comes out of my account with ZERO glitches.  I will look to switch carriers. Definitely read the reviews on T-Mobile. I believe 10% are great, but 84% are a 1. That tells you all you need to know. Hopefully you don’t get suckered in to giving T-Mobile your hard earned money.
I went 3 times to T-Mobile to change SIM card, I wasted about four hours and got no where and they still didn’t change it. I am very disappointed with them.

Chirstmas Eve I decide to buy my son an Iphone for doing exceptionally well during the school year. About 5 employees on staff over 15 people waiting. A rep name "Ophelia" at the Tmobile store on Golf and Milwaukee tells me that the phone i'm looking for is a available to purchase and activate as a NEW LINE. She tells me to come back in 45min and we can get started activating the phone. Unwraps the phone out of the plastic while she's giggling with the dude employees and not really focusing on her job nor answering any of my questions properly. At this point she has made her 3rd mistake now in terms of quality and efficiency, but I am a nice gal. Its Christmas right? Shes taking almost another 45minutes in the break room. Ophelia then brings her manager out to talk to Me. Anthony, who comes out looking like he was back there napping the 2 hours I haven't seen this guy AT ALL since being here.(Nice!) tuck in your shirt dude and wear tmobile gear!!! Starts putting up his hand and not allowing me to ask any questions and begins telling me she activated a display phone that can't be sold. They call a store in Rosemont, and tells me that this is the only store that carry's the phone I want ---they will hold it and wait for me out of the inconvenience and when I got there the Mannheim Rod location now is saying they have no iphone 12pro Max  and never spoke to anyone at the Golf Road location. THE WORST TEAM/MANAGEMENT experience in my 15 years of customer service.  I have actiavted new line with AT&T and they were all so effieicnt, informative and prepared. TMOBILE It is disappointing to have this experience with your Golf Road, Skokie office especially working with sick patients all day and looking for a new phone for my son on Christmas eve after a long day at work.

I had been with Sprint 20 years. When T-Mobile merged at first their service was great. Then they stop doing some things that helped service in rural areas and their service went down.hill— simultaneously, their customer service went downhill I was sent back-and-forth between customer service on the phone and in the store. I was told I had to go to the store, then when I got to the store they looked at me like I was from outer space and didn’t even try to help just walked away.
Decided I don’t have to put up with this! So glad I switch to AT&T.— AT&T has great service in my rural area. Their customer service has been above and beyond several times. When I order new devices they actually send an expert right to my door and make sure that everything is just as I want it. When I needed an international calling plan they had a fantastic one and even backdated it to cover a call I had already made.

Sprint wasn't the greatest company in the world but now they merged with T-Mobile I'm ready to give up. I've been with Sprint for over 10 years. Then they told me I had to upgrade to the 5g network. My phone worked just fine but they made me change anyway through a T-Mobile store that was close to where I live. Since TMobile been in the picture everything went down including customer service and the service of the network. I won't go through all the bad because there's too much to list. They are a totally crooked company now. Be ware Stay away from them. If your thinking about changing to them because of their Ads.. DON'T! They have my whole account messed up and the so called upgrade was a downgrade by around $300 value of phone. They sold me an equivalent of a toy phone you would give your 10 year old. Their return policy is 14 days in the same store you bought it in. I've been there twice to return the overly price toy and they refused to let me make the return. The return policy is in black and white at the bottom of the receipt. The last time I was in there I left swear ing and so angry I thought my head would bust. They're all young kids running the store and they have NO adult supervision. There's a lot more problems just not enough time in the day to mention all. Stay away

I cancelled my T-Mobile acct in November 2021. As soon as I switched out the SIM cards I couldn’t access my acct. I went to their store and said they couldn’t find my acct. So honestly, I was like ok if they can’t find it I should be ok. So today, December 24th I was charged my normal fee. I called the customer service number and they said I was still active. They also said that since I never CALLED customer service I never ended our agreement. So apparently today is my official last day and they said they have the right to charge me for all of December. I started crying, I work 6 days a week paycheck to paycheck I can’t take a $200 hit to my checking again. They didn’t care. So FYI for those wanting to cancel T-Mobile please CALL customer service. Or better yet don’t sign up!!!

Thanks for all the reviews. I was just about to accept their promotional offer of paying off my current phones to my provider, ATT. My wife suggested I look at the reviews first. Glad I did. I will not be switching over to T-Mobile based on all these negative reviews. They were very helpful.

I have been with T mobile for almost two years and today was the last time I will deal with them again all they do is lie and rip people off. They don’t care about there customers and I am moving to any mobile service provider I don’t even care if I pay more for service but I will never go thru this and be treated like I am not good enough to have there service. Please don’t use them you will be so unhappy in the end.

I think we all should call the news station and let them no just how bad this company is I bought 3 phones and they don’t work I almost lost my job because of it and they don’t care and I still owe 600 left on it and I have called every day for 6 months and still nothing I’m just going to go to the store and put the phones on the table and leave and just not pay for them but I no some one that is on the news so I think I’m going to call them so no one else has to go though what I am if anyone wants to talk let me no and please tell everyone you no not to go there t mobile sucks they are very bad

I wish 0 stars was an option. Ever since the merge with sprint the service has been nothing but awful. We’ve been back to the store countless times. My mom has been through three phones and now her voicemail will not set up. She cannot make any phone calls, and if someone calls her it says the account isn’t paid, even though our balance is zero! And messages appear as “message blocking is active” which is not anything we did in settings. Totally awful service and nobody in customer service can help, if you can even get ahold of someone…

Dec 9th, 2021 I went to a T-Mobile store to move my land line phone number to a T-Mobile cell phone and also transfer my current cell phone from another carrier.
I was supposed to get these two lines for $70 / mo with a senior discount.
They set me up with a new (low end) 5G phone for the land line and were suppose to transfer my other number. On Dec 17th I went online to schedule an appointment to make sure that my phone numbers had been properly transferred.  Today I showed up 10 minutes early for my 1:30  pm appointment and waited 30 minutes to see a representative who told me that my request to port my land line had been cancelled.  They didn't transfer my other number either.  I told them to cancel everything.  They refunded the disconnect charges of $60 and charged me $20 to restock their phone which I never used.  Then I was instructed to verify with my original carrier that my old cell phone number was still active on that account, which I did.
Then I was told to call T-Mobile customer service to cancel the 2 new accounts.  An hour or more later I was informed that my account will be cancelled Jan 9th, and no charges would be applied to my account. The woman who processed the cancellation said she would send me a text message guaranteeing my account was cancelled.  I have not received that text.  The whole process was a total CLUSTER F!  IF YOU WANT THE ABSOLUTE WORST CUSTOMER SERVICE AND WAIT TIMES TO SPEAK TO SOMEONE THEN BY ALL MEANS GET A T-MOBILE PHONE/S.  GLAD TO BE DONE WITH T-MOBILE - HOPEFULLY!!!!!

I cancelled my service a few months ago because of how terrible the coverage was.  I had to try 3 or 4 times to finish any phone call.  I went into a store and was basically told they wouldn't help me because I didn't have an active account and would have to call the 800#.  FYI, anytime you call their 800#, even the one on the bill, you will wait an hour...at least.  I was expecting a final bill of around $120 which i did receive a bill for. Then I got one for $205.  Trying to get clarification on why the difference, and was told by the 800 # to go into a store. Store won't help because I don't have an active account.  Wow! This company stinks!!!! Do yourself a favor and go to any other provider but them for cell phone service.

edits.text: Worst phone service..EVER. Starting having issues in 2011 and when I tried to find out what the issue was, I was told the fires burned down towers and there slowly repairing them.  Ok, I'll accept that.  Service in my area progressively got worse.  I was NEVER able to finish a phone call without trying at least 5 times.  I went into a store (what a joke) and was given a cell spot which never worked and didnt improve the quality of the calls.  This was 8 years ago.  Never received any kind of warning or anything, and now they are charging $150 for it not being returned. I paid over $250 a month for phone service that I couldn't complete a call on, and now they want to return something from 8 years ago. Do yourself a favor and do NOT go with this company.

text:
Untrained CSR's poorly designed web site and app. Really missing sprint about now. With all its warts.
TMobile is terrible!!!

I have been with T-Mobile just about they started. The service was not at all good the customer service was amazing. Now they are just as bad as Verizon. Have to wait 2 hours to get someone . In the meantime they sent me a message that an authorized user bought a phone. There are 2 of us that can make changes. Me, the original one and the other one is deceased so.....why do I have to wait due to your error? I never thought after all these years and countless people who I brought to this company that they would become one of those companies.

Called customer service to see why I received a full bill because I previously called on 12.5.21 to cancel services and Cecily stated that billing date closes on 12.20.21. I spoke with Brittany on 12.20.21 she stated that all billing services are 2 months behind in which I think that’s B…S…
If you want to steal money from your consumer’s, please make sure the everyone’s on the same page.
I was a customer with T-Mobile for over 20 years and after this billing issue,I want be returning.

Canceled service 7 months ago. Called today t Mobil keeps charging me. Spoke with Jonathan who left me on hold for over 1 hour. He did tell me account was cancelled. I ask why t Mobil keeps billing me. He could not answer. Save yourself the trouble use any other provider 
Horrible customer service and any service.
Should be ashamed how dishonest they are

From day 1, it never worked right.
From the moment I signed up I was only getting 0.1 mbps. For next 4 days, I spent an average of 2 hours on hold to talk to customer service. Only to be given useless suggestions like "trying turning your phone off and on and wait 24 hours". Nothing was ever fixed. And after a lot of complaining, I finally got them to cancel my account and promise me a refund.

But two months later, they are still billing me each month for service I don't have!
And they tell me that I can't connect the billing department, because I no longer have an account. Yet they keep billing as if I do. They actually told me if I want my money back or to even get them to stop billing me, I would have to take it up with their legal team. I am shocked, I have seen scam artists who are less corrupt than t-mobile.

After almost 20 years with T-Mobile, the last year or two have been really disappointing with their so called "Experts" who are not experts by any means, but customer service agents who don't know anything about their own product. Aside from getting fortunate enough to be routed to a call center in Missouri once, just about every time I call, I get routed to an outsourced center. The US agent was really good. But the outsourced agents are all horrible. Even when chatting once it was an outsourced agent. Time and time again, all I get is wrong information or agents doing wrong things to my account and messing up my account or wasting my time. I spent over an hour with one agent who's main resolution was for me to keeping downloading and deleting the app. Then in her notes she put I declined her help, which was a lie. She just couldn't help me so I asked her to escalate after an hour of wasting my time with her.  I used to really like the customer service and now when I call, the outsource agents have no idea what I'm talking about because they don't know their own products. The company has totally gone downhill, whether Covid or merger with Sprint, not sure why.

Ever since T-Mobile merged with Sprint, NOTHING works right.
-	A few days ago I opened a new line to get a phone for my daughter and today they messaged me the phone number, with an area code in a DIFFERENT STATE
-	When I immediately called customer service, the message said it will take “over 2 hours” for a response, so I opted for them to call me back. 4 ½ hours later, after midnight, it called back, then put me on hold with terrible, screeching "music" for another 10 minutes, then a customer service rep named "Lady" hung up on me almost immediately and didn’t call back
-	Now I’ve been on their “live chat” for an hour with no “live” response 
-	Everything asks for my 6-digit PIN, which I was never issued
-	The app keeps changing to Spanish, even though I’ve only ever done business with them in English
-	Even the support hotline for the managers at the physical stores takes over an hour to respond, and always with a technician who can’t understand English, so the store employees have to repeat even numbers over and over again
-	A few months ago they sold me a smart watch that can’t do what they said it could, and they messed up how they set up the account, too.  So I spent over 12 hours at the store or on the phone to finally get the watch returned & removed, and still never refunded over $80 I initially paid for the setup
-	And many, many more examples
T-Mobile do yourself a favor.15 people waiting in line for simple service issues is just not good customer service.As well as adding chairs for the ridiculous wait times!

I would like a commend Matt C.  He was very helpful and cool.  One of the nicest people I’ve met at a phone store, this guy needs a promotion.  

Store 2SJO 1391 34th st N st Pete FL

I have been with T-Mobile for 19 years, 9 months and I'm now on my last 24 hours before I switch to a new provider.

Over those almost 20 years, I have paid then $42,000.

I purchased a T Mobile Plus One 6T and it has been nothing but awful and been replaced three times.

When it last malfunctioned and I was out of the country, it cut off calls, was unable to send or receive text messages and my WhatsApp that I used for friends in this country totally malfunctioned.

I came back to the USA, spent close to 5 hours on the phone with multiple techs and it worked 50% better, totally unacceptable.  

They offered me a new phone, of course it was $300 cheaper, without the same GBs of my bad boy phone and the tech reminded that phones were not free.  I think after $42,000, I'm not free either.

For the fourth time they sent me a refurbished phone, which I told them I didn't want but they were willing to sell me a new phone for $700 or $800 dollars.

No mas, if you are looking for a company that disrespects loyalty, treats long term customers like they don't matter, do sign up for T Mobile.

I'm fed up and have paid them enough to be treated respectfully.

Ciao T Mobile, you suck,!

I received an email to get magenta max and trade in my iPhone for a iphone13. Also free air pods. There is nothing free in this offer according to the customer service agents I spoke with. Don’t waste my time

Be careful! Do not trust what the representatives tell you as they do not even know their own policies. Thoroughly read the contracts. I was trying to move over from ATT Wireless bringing my own phones. The rep. told me they would not start billing until I activated the SIM cards. Unfortunately, they started billing the day they arrive when I did not have time to activate them for a few weeks. After many many hours and 4 phone calls later over a couple of months I gave up and paid what they stated I owed even when I did not use the SIM cards. The last rep. even sarcastically told me to have a nice day and laughed at me before hanging up. BTW, the first two reps. hung up on me. At no time was I a jerk and stayed professional through all the calls without even raising my voice.

T mobile customer service assisted me in making an insurance claim for my damaged phone.  Only they filled out the wrong claim info, forcing me to pay out of pocket for my phone. 
I have been paying insurance for nothing.  They refuse to take responsibility for the error costing me $700.
I will be looking for a company who takes care of their 15 + yr customers better
This Company lies to try to get new customers to switch to them by sending texts saying your old service is being retired. I have proof and plan on taking legal action

Been with Sprint for 20 years and all has been well. Decided to spend two weeks in Italy and we personally went to a Sprint/T Mobile to confirm we would have data service. In the Covid world we wanted to be as contactless as possible. It was confirmed, but we did not have it. When we  discovered this we spent two hours of our vacation speaking to the customer service person. He walked us through everything and finally gave up. Sprint/T Mobile is useless in Europe evidently. If we knew this we would have made other arrangements, but now it was too late. DO not use this carrier if you want data service in Europe and do not trust their representatives know what they are talking about.

I had 2 phones on my account, after my daughter moved out she got her own T Mobile account, and I had her old phone removed from my account, I contacted the store and customer service to find out every detail that would be done for the disconnect, well not paying attention to my bill(autopay) i found out that paid $344 for the Iphone 11 equipment charge, i did say i asked about every detail with the disconnect right? this somehow was not brought up until I saw the charges, I will go to Wal-Mart and buy a phone before I go back to another large money hungry cell phone company.

The 3stars go to our rep! He was fantastic! T mobile deserves a big zero! We have the phones for 2 days. Most calls dropped or no service. We are in a large city. We had Verizon for over 12+ years. Never dropped calls or no bars even outside standing in our street! Headed back to T mobile to return the phones and then to Verizon to get reliable phone service.

They're scam artists!!! The ONLY reason I signed up was they told me I was eligible for the EBB program, two months later,they have told me I have to RE APPLY, which I did, I was approved I even have an application ID, T mobile has now said THEY would let me know in a week or two if I've been approved!!!!??? My bill as it happens is due Saturday!! AND this is the THIRD time I've "applied"...SCAM!!!! After Christmas I'll be canceling

I've been with Sprint for 16 years. When T mobile bought Sprint they told me my current phones would not work and I had to turn in my great LG8 phones that were paid for so I could get service still. They told me they would not work starting Jan 1st. I let them switch my phones, now my phones rarely work and I have to go outside to talk to people on them. They also added a "tracker" to my account they said would not cost anything- lie!. Everytime I call them, they lie to me. I'm not waiting another minute on the phone with them, many hrs dealing with this. My bill went from 230 to 468/ 3 phones. Maybe they will realize I canceled my account when they don't get paid! 🤔 
Sincerely discussed customer.

My experience is so extremely horrible and I can’t even explain anymore, it’s wore me out! Nothing but lies and stealing from this company! I really want to know if there is a class action suit or how to sue this company! Please if anyone has any experience with this I need to know?! This nightmare feels like it’s never going to end and this company needs to be held accountable for its dishonest criminal behavior! In Washington State

Back it July I called up three to order TMobile after the first person I talked to and going over the information found out he don't even know the prices after called again to tell them I didn't want their service cause the can't get things straight and after tying for months for them to send me a shipping lable and finally returned the phone the end of September that's how long it took them to track down my account and mail me the lable and in October I called for a refund and her it is December and they put me in collections for 500 dollars for what the phone was returned no so on hold with them now and it's been 2 hours for them to find my dam account I had to call the sales line just to get a live person and now we're both on hold with customers service trying to find out why we're in collections for a phone that was returned I told to my lawyers they told me I have a very very strong cause and i can sue them and win so that's what I'll be doing if this don't get straightened out I hate to do that but this is nuts if they hurt my credit score you better bet I'm sueing I have never seen this kind of incompetence

My wife had/has sprint/t mobile and she has hated it since t mobile took over. She has joined my family plan with AT&T and is very happy with their service. So she has tried to cancel her plan with sprint and has become so frustrated with them that she wants me to handle it. She is easily frustrated, so I just thought that if I did it, it would get done that day with relative ease. I very rarely get frustrated to the point where I will be willing to openly bad mouth a company. But I have tried to cancel her phone plan for her, and it seems like it is set up to be impossible to cancel the plan. I have called the local stores who apparently are not permitted to handle cancellations and they have given me six different numbers to call, five of which either can’t take phone calls or the line is busy. The sixth number is automated and it wanted to know what number I am calling about. So I tell it, and it says that that number is not in their records so it cannot help me. Because sprint is merging with t mobile, sprint wants me to speak with t mobile to handle this. So I go to t mobile and they say, “Oh, it looks like you are a sprint customer. You need to call them.” So it just goes into an endless circle. My wife is still being billed for their services, which she is no longer using. So I told her to call her bank and tell them that anything that is coming from sprint or t mobile is now to be considered fraud. Maybe they will give us a call or they will just cancel it on their own. We are done with them.

Was swapped from Sprint too T- Not So Mobile, ever since switching too the T Mobile SIM card, my internet service sucks, and have dropped calls CONSTANTLY!!!!! Won't choice Sprint had ever made and I was a customer of Sprint since right after they got MCI. I haven't had this bad of cellular service since analog phones were out. I'd give them a 0 star if possible but you can't. Definitely looking for a new service provider. Oh and their "SUPER FAST 5 G"  service is worse than dial up. The company I work for uses Verizon and I can't stand it. But it's better than what I'm getting with T Mobile now.

I bought a prepaid plan, traded in my iphone for a promotional rate. I knew I had 14 days so if there were any problems, I'd cancel. Nothing but problems; they could not get my voice mail to work was the biggest. After hours on the phone I cancelled after 10 days. They told me the iphone I traded in was gone and that I would have to eat the value of it. It was  worth a good $500. The store rep was entirely unhelpful with processing my return/cancellation and told me to call in to cancel. The phone rep said only the store could canel me. Finally got it done at the store. Had to fight with the manager to waive the restocking fees for the devices I returned. Finally relieved to be done with it. A couple weeks later they start billing me and saying I owe them $300 for unpaid balance. Hours on the phone with them and now I'm getting letters from a collections agency. Stay away, this company is a train wreck.

WHAT A DISHONEST PHONE COMPANY!! Called T-Monbile (800-937-8997) on 12/14/21 at 11:36 AM. Rico answered and gave me a quote for 3 lines (unlimited text, talk, & data) on New Senior 55+ Promotion.  3 lines for $90 per month total (including all fees and taxes) & 3 iPhone 13 phones for no charge if we turned in our IPhone Xr. There was one additional charge of $5 per line for phone protection.  NO CONTRACT REQUIRED.  Good Deal.  Placed my order, the confirmation number was 110117207439.  When I received my contract by e-mail, the monthly charge was $284.01 PER MONTH FOR 30 MONTHS.
I called to protest the change in charges and was switched from Roman to  Martin to Erica (ID#8121503), to  Ren (ID#8042730) who each quoted a different number that they thought I might pay. After 1:34 minutes on the phone without anyone being able to send me a corrected contract, I cancelled the order.  Ren advised that T-Mobile would not accept cancellation and I advised that I had cancelled the order and did not care whether they would accept it or not.  My cancellation call was within 2 hours of the placement phone call.  My initial call to obtain a quote was at 11:36 AM on 12/14/21.  My call to cancel was at 12:22 PM on 12/14/21.  
Please contact me if you have any questions or would like to have more details about T-Mobiles dishonest dealings.

T-Mobile has scammed me through this method.  Upon buying a new phone, they offered me a tablet, "free" with no charges except for one small one time connection fee and tax.  I bought it for $50.  It doesn't work.  Then I find out to my surprise my bill is being charged $16 extra for this device I can't use every month.  Multiple calls to their T-Mobile "Experts" in their Indonesian call center, cut-off by staff who are not fluent in English and can offer no help.  My choices are either to stay with T-Mobile and pay to be ripped off or change carriers.

T-mobile company lied to me telling me the price of the I-phone 13 is $806.00 and Charged the amount in full and charged me another installment fee for $68.00. That was not enough, they bought back my I-phone 6 for $100.00 and when I expected a credit on my bill, they gave me $3.17 on installment credit to be paid with in the next 30 months. When I asked for my phone back they said its already refurbished and shipped to make more money for them, perhaps 3 fold or more money.

I do not understand how I can pay for unlimited data and still get overage notices. I just got another one this morning. I do not understand why the billing practices are this way. Last time this happened I got a "oh wow you were not on the right plan" when I thought I was. So I changed everything and spent yet more money with them. Now here we are again almost a few years later with them telling me I'm going to run out of data. 

I really don't have anything more to say about them, the billing practices really do have a scummy vibe to them. 

Both my wife and I are starting to feel taken advantage of and it's getting to the point where we are starting to look at alternatives. 

T-Mob, you can do better then this. If $$$ is going to get the customer unlimited then just tell them that. If unlimited doesn't really exist on your network then stop calling it that.  This isn't right.

10 month old Samsung phone lost its screen, went to the T-mobile store on Friday. They said a warentee phone would be in Saturday, it didn't. Tried contacting customer service, automated system said they would call back in 30 minutes. They did but I could not answer the phone or press number keys with a screen that worked. Got in contact with store and was told they couldn't help. Got an email that the replacement phone would be in Monday, phone didn't show. I missed phone calls from Moffit cancer center and Tampa General Hospital, the service is unsatisfactory.

T Mobile is the absolute worst provider. DO NOT USE THEM! They straight out lied about BOTH the package services AND the price. They have the worst service I have ever had across all lines of business. They have on multiple occasions cut our service for a "unfortunate mistake with the system". PLEASE DO YOURSELF A FAVOR AND CHOOSE ANY OTHERN PROVIDER

Terrible incompetent customer service. Never received my upgrade in the mail and got charged $804.72 and the stores can't help on anything, have to call customer service on everything. Been trying to get refunded since October. Hope they get sued, or go out of business like Enron, and Circuit City. This is unacceptable.

If I could give less than one I would
The whole free phone thing is a scam!
Oh and getting you to pay your phone off today in order to move on or give a quote is total BS. Been on the phone and at store all day and have not done anything productive but get scammed! Still trying to get my refund!

Was told I would owe nothing on my phone (2 of them) because of trade-ins.  Then, after the two week buyer's remorse period is over they tell me oh there is a payment on the phones, it must not have been explained properly AND, if I pay phone off early, they will upcharge me for the original price of the phones and not have to give our phones back.  Shisters, crooks.  Lie cheat steal.  They are bad.  Stay far away.

I ordered a free smartphone, 5-g and a gateway modem. Not one thing worked. The modem was delivered wrong the phone was not 5g and 2 months into the billing 200 300 and hrs. And days on experts  This has destroyed my credit rating. And I cannot even use the equipment.. Worst experience of my life.

I updated my sprint phone 12/3/21 to migrate with T-Mobile for the new i13 in pink. I was told two days I would receive it. Sprint sent two text messages asking about the phone. I reached out to T-Mobile 12/5,12/7, 12/8,12/9 and now I’m told the phone is back ordered. There was no disclaimer on the website stating this. Each time I’d call T-Mobile they world tell me to call back tomorrow. Finally ups sent an email saying the phone would be delivered by 12/8/21 at 9:00 pm. I received another message saying Estimated delivery
The delivery date will be provided as soon as possible. Worse service ever

I’d give less than 1 star if I could!  I am / was a Sprint customer who decided to transition to TMobile.. naively thinking it would be a smooth transition. WRONG!! After offering my wife and I a seemingly decent plan and promotion.. all went downhill after that on numerous levels from not offering any support or help in either transitioning the old phones to new phones and also making it extremely difficult to return the old phones.  Numerous times over three days I spent hours on holds or waiting for call-backs that would never happen. 
When they finally took 1 of my old phones (wouldn’t take the other one at the store and insisted I needed to get a shipping label just for that one.. but wouldn’t supply it at the store.. leading to another 3 hour plus wait again @ 611.. a day later T-mobile cancelled my promotion telling me I turned in an iPhone 6 instead of the iPhone 8 I turned in. Cue now hours waiting and arguing on 611 insisting I turned in an iPhone 6.. again I did not. 
Then.. a day later and more hours wasted on 611.. after 3 days the story changed to it’s not an iPhone 6 but it was an iPhone 8 and it had water damage and now that’s why the promotional offer is now cancelled. In my window to cancel my contract I asked to do so.. and they will not return my old phone. 
I am looking to file against TMobile in small claims court to try to recover my old phone..if I do not hear from their legal department after faxing and asking for immediate customer arbitration. ..I have also emailed the CEO of TMobile.. but again as of today no response from anyone @ TMobile.. 
with T-Mobile it’s always your fault, you the customer don’t know what yuu is re taking about, and their story about why it’s your fault changes every day. 
TMobile.. don’t do it!!  You’ve been warned.

I decided to try T-Mobile due to the free phone offer but then reading these reviews decided to return the phone and cancel.  I never even activated the service but to cancel I was first argued with about why I should really keep the service what great phone and tablet they were ect... I advised of the worst reviews I have ever seen for any business.  Still he kept on and on in a stern manner.  Fineally transferred me to someone to cancel the line even though I told him I didnt have a line setup yet.  Hold Hold Hold like forever just to go through it all again and be transferred again as oops, this rep couldnt cancel without a line....yep.   Then the rep I got went through it all again kept saying I'm working on it then suddenly the hold music comes on as in the beginning of the call!!  Holding again only to finally be answered and yep, starting all over again.  Howeveer apparently the previous rep had canceled the account then put me back on hold....yes, Really!! Never Ever EVER!!! T-Mobile absolutely NOT... Call and hold times 1 Hour 8 minutes and 25 seconds!

I just got my new phone from T mobile. I call T Mobile phone support and say, can you activate this phone? First guy hung up on me because he had no idea what he was doing. What kind of training does T Mobile provide to their employees? This is really upsetting. I'm going to have to go to a T Mobile Store to get my setup completed.

I personally don't know or understand what All this talk about TMoble Not providing Excellent support. We have been with Sprint for over 24 years now with the TMoble merger and have received 😀 the most Amazing service from TMoble/Sprint customer service staff,  both located her in the USA & offshore. Very Special Personal understanding of our needs. Spectacular Service.

T-mobile is blaming poor 5g service on current service being done in the State of Florida.  I asked if the 5g service problems involve the continental United States and they said Texas, California, Florida, and one other state I don't recall.  Of course while callers can't hear me, including T-Mobile, I pass the signs in Titusville on SR 50 of Best 5G coverage in Florida.  Dropped service 4 times in an 8 minute period and the caller has a T-mobile phone and account.  Frustrating how awful their service is within the city limits of Jacksonville, Florida, not the woods...I had service after Metro PCS in 2014 and T-mobile had no service in south St. Johns County on I-95 and no service on the Beeline Expressway west of I-95.  Want me to use LTE rather than 5G. Horrible.

author.name: Bankruptcy Law Office of Michael J. Duggar

text:
T-Mobile is terrible in the way doing its business, very untrustworthy and uncountable.  It does not only give empty promises but also provides fake information to customers.  It may have the best price, but it has traps waiting ahead.

I was a new customer joining T-Mobile this October when they had a buy-one-get-one-free phone promotion.  The new iPhone was hard to order when it was first released, and I was advised by T-Mobile local shop to call its support for ordering.  When I called,  a T-Mobile sales representative told me I could pay one phone in full and the other free from the promotion.  After switching to T-mobile, I asked for documents on what I signed up for;  I waited forever and never received what I was looking for.  Within a week of opening the account with T-Mobile, I called to check my account status, and I was told that I had to pay my two phones at the full price, and I wasn't getting the buy-one-get-one-free phone promotion.  Back and ford, I called many times, and each time I was told they would take care of my account, and whenever I called back again, the promotion was still not applied.  I told them I could return my phones and plan to end this fraud.  I spoke with many T-mobile supports and managers, who kept telling me they would take care of me and the issue.  After my last call last month, I saw justification on my phone bill.  Unfortunately, all the promises I received were fake and lies.  After my phones' return date was expired, I saw my monthly payment for the promised buy-one-get-one-free phone was now remoted from my account.  On a recent call to T-Mobile, a manager told me she was so sorry for their mistakes, but it was solely my responsibility; they won't do anything for me.  This is like, "sorry, we made a mistake, it is ok for us to lie, but you screw and take the blames"!  This manager also told me I shouldn't trust what their sales representatives said to me on the phone.  Wow, unbelievable!

Do not trust T-mobile; they are frauds. Don't do business with an untrustworthy entity!

Stay away from these jerk offs they promised you a plan take your phones then they tell you your bill is going to be twice what the contract was that you agreed on I told him I wanted my old phones back and I would give them their phones back they refused to give me my phone back so I kept theirs so now I got two brand-new thousand-dollar phones and there's nothing they can do about it don't use T-Mobile

I would have given T-nightmare (mobile) a no star rating if they had it. I was very happy with Sprint service & plan, I was switched to T-mobile without telling us or explaining any of T-mobile plans, It has been nothing but a Nightmare and dropping calls with T-mobile's service their computers system are always down they are having issues with their own computers service how is T-mobile going to resolve any issues their customer's are having with their cell service or data service, I want my Sprint service back or I'm switching to another carrier who will honor all deals & promotion and has real 5G service, T-mobile has been a nightmare they didn't honor any of the deals or promotion the have every T-mobile representative all are saying different things when it comes to their deals and promotions, I am switching to another carrier... ASAP

Had to spell "Milwaukee" 20 times because they are from the Philippines and they have no idea where we all ARE! Their computer system keeps going down and the calls keep getting disconnected. They take f... o... r... e... v... e...r to take down your order and keep pushing you to buy extra things as if their life depends on it. I was on the phone with them for 4 hours today and all I needed was a device upgrade.

I just upgraded my to the iPhone 13 and had several issues getting the phone to work properly. I went to two store and chatted with online tech support for over two hours. I went to two stores and no one was either willing or able to help me.  The very last store I went into was in Rosemount, MN and David fixed my phone in under 5 minutes.  It really boils down to people and training.  Thank you David!

I was with Sprint for years and it was a good service for me. As soon as they told me to put in a T Mobile sim card when they merged with Tmobile my service stopped working. They told me they would give me 2 free months service in order to see if it would work soon. Instead, they back charged me for those months after the 2 months was up. On top of that, they said it would only be a few dollars to send me a mobile hot spot device so I decided to try it. Not only did it not work, they charged me way more for it than they said they would, and worst of all, they put me in a 2 year contract with them for getting the device. No one mentioned this contract whatsoever or I would never have done it. So very frustrated with this company!

SCAMMERS.  They tell you one thing then do another.  Nothing is ever given in writing, when they tell you your old iPhone is worth $200 in-trade then only credit you $75 a month later, "it's your problem not theirs".  Took half a dozen trips to their store for a simple upgrade because they kept screwing everything up.  Incompetent inexperienced help, who just make stuff up as they go, and who haven't a clue what they are doing.
False advertising free for new and existing customers flip phones only offered 400 for 1000 phones. Been with Sprint since 2004. Will go to AT&T

Hold time is terrible 12-1-2021 after on hold for an 1 hour and 3 in total for issue . I thought my issue was resolved NOT called back 12-2-2021 another long hold time and spoke Manager Johnathan again thought my issue was resolved. I was not and Johnathan  was suppose to call today 12-5-2021 at 1:00. I never received a call. I called again now I have been on 52 :53 so far. This T-Mobile customer is not happy. They need to get a new marketing Ad.

Sprint was a pretty good company. I have been with them over 20 years. Think when they first started.Now that they are now part of T Mobile I hate this company. Issues after issues. Now issue with a rebate I did almost a month ago. Giving me the run around and I’m getting tired or dealing with them.

Went to T-Mobile and bought a new scre3n protector for my iPhone 12 . Purchased it from the same store. They installed it. Looked great. For 3 weeks , then it started peeling off . Cost me 43 dollars. Not cheap. I also bought an 800 dollar Samsung phone and a Samsung smart watch for my wife the same week . Paid cash. When I took the iPhone in to get the screen protector fixed that they installed 3 weeks earlier . They told me they couldn’t do anything about it unless I had a paid service protection plan. Shame on you T-Mobile.

I was with sprint for more than 20 years and never had an issue that wasn't dealt with quickly and easily. If you are looking at moving to Tmobile service - RUN.

They switched service and honestly treat their customers like we are lucky to have their service. Patronizing, condescending. Cell service has been horrendous. Then I get a message saying we need to move all of our lines to the new tmobile and one of our lines was not 5g eligible. I order a new 2k phone with a sim card. It arrives without the sim card - in fact, an empty bubble packet marked sim card. When I get on chat I go through 15 minutes of upsells and then I am told if I want the sim card - to be clear, the one ordered with the phone and is MIA- I have to pay to have it overnighted or it's 5 days. So, I can't use my new phone or the old one for 5 days? I had to go and buy a new sim card in order to activate my new phone. They also told me that one of my other lines had to be ported in-person to tmobile. I have my dad on my account who is elderly and handicapped but also lives 3k miles away. I hire someone to take him to the tmobile store and they tell him they can't help him. As the account owner I have to cancel his phone, order a new sim card, and then take him back to the store. I get on chat with tmobile and it goes downhill quickly. More than 20 minutes of patronizing upselling - hey guess what? He qualifies for $600 off the new iPhone 11! I said he doesn't want to change phones right now. Oh, well, he won't be able to connect to the network if he doesn't switch. In other words if you don't switch all of your phones to new super expensive phones due to our merger, none of your phones will work. Awesome. My elderly handicapped father can't access his phone.....
I am now looking for another company to switch this week.
